Steps: Open app. Tap 'Sign in with Google'. Select a Google account from system chooser.
Expected Result: Google account picker opens natively. User signed in and redirected to /billing (or /shop-setup). Profile loaded.

Test Name: Google sign-in popup-blocked fallback on web
Steps: On web browser with popup blocker active, trigger Google sign-in.
Expected Result: Redirect fallback activates automatically. Auth succeeds. No blank screen or stuck state.

Steps: After registration on Android, enter 10-digit phone. Tap 'Send OTP'.
Expected Result: Real SMS received on phone. 6-digit OTP entered. Phone linked to account.

Steps: Launch Windows app. Note 8-char link code. Click 'Sign In via Browser'. Complete Google sign-in in opened browser page.
Expected Result: Browser shows 'You can close this window'. Desktop app auto-signs in via custom token within seconds.

Steps: Sign in with email. Go to Settings > Account. Tap 'Link Google Account'. Complete native Google flow.
Expected Result: Google shows as 'Linked' with checkmark. Both providers available for future logins.

Steps: Go to Account > Danger Zone. Tap 'Delete Account'. Confirm. Type 'DELETE'.
Expected Result: Account deleted via Cloud Function. User signed out. All Firestore data scheduled for deletion. Cannot re-login.

Steps: In POS, tap barcode scanner icon. Point camera at a physical barcode that exists in inventory.
Expected Result: Camera opens. Barcode read. Product auto-added to cart. Scanner closes.

Steps: Point camera at a barcode NOT in inventory.
Expected Result: Snackbar: 'Product not found' with 'Add' action. Tapping 'Add' opens add-product with barcode pre-filled.

Steps: Scan 10 different physical barcodes back-to-back in one POS session.
Expected Result: Each scan resolves correctly. Camera does not freeze or lag. No duplicate scan events.

Steps: In Add Product modal, tap barcode scanner icon. Scan a physical barcode.
Expected Result: Barcode field auto-filled. If found in catalog: product name auto-populated.

Test Name: UPI payment link sent via WhatsApp
Steps: Complete UPI bill with customer selected. Tap 'Send Payment Link'.
Expected Result: WhatsApp opens with pre-filled payment link to customer's phone number.

Steps: Complete a bill with customer who has phone. Tap WhatsApp icon in success dialog.
Expected Result: WhatsApp app opens with pre-filled bill details message to customer's number.

Steps: Complete a bill. Tap 'Print' in success dialog.
Expected Result: Connected printer produces physical receipt: shop name, bill #, items, totals, payment method, footer.

Steps: Create the 3rd bill on a fresh account.
Expected Result: Android/iOS native in-app review dialog appears after bill success.

Steps: Open POS on: (a) phone (<600px), (b) tablet (600-900px), (c) desktop (>900px).
Expected Result: (a) Grid + sticky bottom cart. (b) 3-col grid + right drawer. (c) Left grid + fixed right cart panel.

Steps: Open customer with due balance + phone. Tap 'Remind' button.
Expected Result: WhatsApp opens with pre-filled reminder: balance amount, payment link (if UPI set). Customer phone pre-selected.

Steps: Same as above but no UPI ID in settings.
Expected Result: WhatsApp message has generic reminder text (no payment link).

Test Name: Send UPI payment link to customer
Steps: Open customer with due balance. Tap 'Link'. UPI ID must be set.
Expected Result: Payment link with correct amount and UPI ID shared via WhatsApp to customer's phone.

Steps: Open customer. Tap 'Pay'. Select 'Online'. Complete Razorpay checkout.
Expected Result: Razorpay native SDK opens. After success: balance reduces atomically. Payment proof saved.

Steps: In Khata tab, tap 'Download Report'.
Expected Result: Text report generated. Native share sheet opens with: total outstanding, customer list, balances.

Test Name: Upgrade to Pro plan via Razorpay
Steps: On web, tap 'Upgrade to Pro'. Complete Razorpay checkout (UPI).
Expected Result: Razorpay modal opens with correct amount. After payment: plan upgrades. Limits increased.

Steps: Start upgrade. In Razorpay modal, press Cancel/Back.
Expected Result: Snackbar: 'Payment cancelled'. Plan unchanged. No charge.

Steps: Start upgrade. Payment fails (insufficient funds).
Expected Result: User-friendly error shown. Plan unchanged.

Test Name: Upgrade redirects to web on Android
Steps: On Android, tap 'Upgrade to Pro'.
Expected Result: Browser opens subscription web page (Play Billing compliance). Payment completes on web.

Steps: Send notification from Super Admin while app is open on Android.
Expected Result: In-app notification shown. Bell badge updates. Notification appears in list.

Steps: Send notification while app is in background/killed on Android.
Expected Result: System notification tray shows notification. Tapping it opens app to relevant route.

Steps: Send notification to Windows user. App is open.
Expected Result: Windows toast notification appears. Clicking routes to relevant screen.

Steps: Go to Hardware > Bluetooth. Tap 'Scan Printers'. Tap 'Link' on discovered printer.
Expected Result: Scanning spinner. Devices listed. Connection succeeds. Status: 'Connected' with printer name.

Steps: Enable auto-print. Connect BT printer. Create and complete a bill.
Expected Result: Receipt auto-prints on BT printer after bill completion. No manual action needed.

Steps: With BT printer connected, tap 'Disconnect'.
Expected Result: Printer disconnected. Status reverts to 'Not connected'.

Steps: Select WiFi printer. Enter IP (e.g. '192.168.1.100') and port (9100). Tap Connect.
Expected Result: Connection succeeds. Test print produces physical output.

Test Name: Windows USB printer discovery and print
Steps: On Windows, go to Hardware > USB. Refresh. Select printer. Click Test Print.
Expected Result: System printers listed. Physical test receipt printed on selected printer.

Steps: Create bill with: long product names (30+ chars), tax enabled, footer text. Print on 58mm and 80mm.
Expected Result: Receipt aligned correctly on both paper sizes. No clipped lines. Footer visible. Totals readable.

Steps: Print same bill on 58mm then 80mm paper setting.
Expected Result: 58mm: Narrower, wrapped text. 80mm: Wider, less wrapping. Both fully legible.

Test Name: UPI app chooser from payment link
Steps: Open generated payment link on Android phone. Tap Pay Now.
Expected Result: System UPI app chooser opens (GPay, PhonePe, Paytm etc.). Correct amount and payee shown.

Test Name: Desktop QR code scanned by phone UPI app
Steps: Open payment page on web. Display QR. Scan from phone UPI app.
Expected Result: QR resolves to correct payee + amount. Payment completable from phone.

Test Name: No UPI app installed on device
Steps: Open payment link on device without any UPI app.
Expected Result: Graceful fallback: guidance text. No crash. Alternative suggestion shown.

Steps: Configure UPI ID in Billing Settings. Tap 'Send ₹1 Test Payment'.
Expected Result: Real ₹1 payment flow initiates via UPI to verify ID is functional.

Steps: Set maintenanceMode=true in Firebase Remote Config. Open app.
Expected Result: Maintenance screen blocks all access. Normal app inaccessible.

Steps: Set minimumVersion higher than current app version in Remote Config. Open app.
Expected Result: Force-update dialog shown. User cannot proceed. Must update app.

Steps: Set announcement text in Remote Config. Open app. Dismiss. Reopen.
Expected Result: Banner appears. After dismissal: does not reappear until new announcement set.

Test Name: Data created on Device A appears on B
Steps: Login on two devices. Create product on Device A.
Expected Result: Product appears on Device B within seconds without manual refresh.

Steps: Open same product on 2 devices. Save different prices within seconds.
Expected Result: No crash or corruption. Last write wins. Both devices converge to same value.

Steps: Turn off WiFi/data. Create a bill. Reconnect.
Expected Result: Bill saved locally with sync badge. After reconnect: badge clears, bill in cloud.

Steps: Disable network. Create 3 bills, add product, record Khata payment. Re-enable network.
Expected Result: All operations succeed locally. Sync badges shown. After reconnect: all data syncs. Badges clear.

Steps: Toggle device network on and off.
Expected Result: 'OFFLINE' banner appears when disconnected. Disappears when reconnected. App usable throughout.

Steps: Build AAB via smart-deploy.ps1. Upload to Google Play Console internal/closed testing.
Expected Result: AAB accepted. No signing or version violations. Release processable.

Steps: Install older build. Publish newer to testing track. Open old build.
Expected Result: In-app update prompt appears. Flexible or immediate based on priority config.

Steps: Generate MSIX via smart-deploy.ps1. Install on clean Windows machine.
Expected Result: MSIX installs successfully. App launches. Auto-update works when newer version available.

Steps: Create Udhar bill. Simulate network drop mid-save.
Expected Result: WriteBatch atomicity: bill + balance + transaction all saved or all fail. No partial state.

Test Name: Razorpay success but Firestore write fails
Steps: Complete Razorpay Khata payment. Simulate Firestore failure.
Expected Result: Payment proof saved locally (crash-safe). 3 retries (2s/4s/6s). Final failure: 'sync later' warning.

Steps: Two devices sell same product simultaneously. Initial stock = 10.
Expected Result: Both use FieldValue.increment(-1). Final stock = 8 (not 9). Atomic operation verified.

Steps: Create bill on Windows desktop.
Expected Result: Bill uses merge-set fallback (not runTransaction). Bill created successfully with correct number.

Steps: Use app 30+ min: bills, products, khata, settings, all screens.
Expected Result: No crash, freeze, severe lag, or memory leak. App stays responsive throughout.

Steps: Load 500 products via CSV. Browse list. Search. Open POS.
Expected Result: List loads <3s. Search responsive. POS grid smooth. No ANR.

Steps: Force-close app. Open from launcher. Time until POS interactive.
Expected Result: Android: <5s. Windows/Web: <3s to interactive POS.

Steps: Enable TalkBack. Navigate: Login → POS → Add to cart → Checkout → Settings.
Expected Result: All buttons/fields announced with meaningful labels. No inaccessible critical actions.

Steps: Set device font to maximum in system settings. Open app.
Expected Result: UI usable. No overflow or overlapping. All buttons reachable.

Steps: Go to Account > Privacy & Data > Download My Data.
Expected Result: JSON downloaded: profile, products, bills, customers, expenses, transactions, settings. File openable and complete.

Steps: On Dashboard, tap PDF/Export icon. Open/print generated PDF.
Expected Result: PDF has: shop branding, period, all 6 stats. Formatted for A4 print. No clipping.

Steps: On Dashboard, tap Share icon.
Expected Result: WhatsApp opens with formatted message: period, total sales, bill count, payment breakdown.

Steps: Tap Export > Last 30 days > CSV. Open downloaded file in Excel.
Expected Result: CSV opens correctly. Columns: bill #, date, items, total, payment. No encoding issues. Record count matches.

Steps: Tap a bill in history. Choose Share > SMS.
Expected Result: SMS app opens with pre-formatted bill text.

Steps: Send broadcast from Admin > Notifications. Check on Android device and Windows app.
Expected Result: Android: FCM notification received. Windows: toast notification received. Both show correct title/body.

Steps: In admin user detail, change subscription plan. User opens their app.
Expected Result: Plan change reflected on user's app. Limits adjusted. Audit trail written.
